mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series shows month-over-month contraction, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening economic momentum and rising corporate output, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent year-over-year or fails to predict returns when INDPRO growth exceeds 2 percent year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is already above 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth falls below 100 thousand or when the joint signal predicts negative returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with expanding corporate profit margins and revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or GDP growth fails to predict returns when inflation exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals weakening economic activity and reduced corporate output, which forces equity markets to price in lower earnings and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent.
